title: Two weeks with our autonomous AI trading agent - results from fresh wallet
author: AttitudeGrouchy33
selftext:
Hey everyone, wanted to share some results from our autonomous trading agent Milo that we've been working on.



We started with a fresh wallet two weeks ago to test our newly deployed pro model with improved reasoning and re-evaluated protocols. The results have been encouraging - 9 active positions, all currently green, with PNL ranging from around 7% to 66%.



Some key learnings:



\- The new reasoning model is making more conservative entry decisions compared to our previous version

\- Risk management improvements are helping avoid the impulsive trades that hurt earlier iterations 

\- The agent posts a full THESIS before each trade showing its analysis (support/resistance, RSI, Bollinger bands, etc), which has been incredibly valuable for understanding its decision-making



Still early days and we know past performance doesn't guarantee future results, but we're cautiously optimistic about the direction. The transparency aspect has been crucial - being able to see exactly why the AI makes each decision removes a lot of the "black box" anxiety.



Happy to answer questions about the approach or share more details about what we've learned building this.



[https://app.andmilo.com/?code=@milo4reddit](https://app.andmilo.com/?code=@milo4reddit)

comments.author: Comfortable_Froyo_72
comments.body:
What does connecting a Wallet mean in your opinion?

 Give permission to my Crypto Wallet to access it?

replies.author: AttitudeGrouchy33
replies.body:
100% agree with you - that's solid advice! Just to clarify, Milo doesn't ask you to connect your existing wallet. Instead, it creates a fresh, self-custodial wallet that you control. You sign in with email/social and it generates a new dedicated wallet for trading.



So you never expose your main wallet. You only deposit what you're comfortable trading with into this new wallet. Same security principle you're recommending - never connect your hot wallet to anything you don't fully trust.

replies.author: Careless-Diamond-277
replies.body: Didnt know,I am just used to connect wallet and get drained

replies.author: AttitudeGrouchy33
replies.body: Well, at least it seems like you learned your lesson sir

replies.author: AttitudeGrouchy33
replies.body:
milo ask to connect wallet which is basically similar to sign in function (he will create a new dedicated self custodial wallet which both of you will have permission to use in order to trade, you would need to deposit funds for it) 

You can use your email or social login to create a fresh one (so you never need to connect your hot wallet) 

like other oter people suggested here, use it as a rule and never connect your wallet to a source you dont know or trust (alwasy do your DD)

comments.author: Paul_Kuhn
comments.body: The bot needs your money to start trading.

comments.author: gardenia856
comments.body: Treat the thesis as data and put hard risk rails in place before you size up. Turn each THESIS into structured fields (regime, entry reason, invalidation level, stop, target, time limit) and tag trades; review hit-rate, expectancy, and max adverse excursion per tag weekly. Run a shadow portfolio that mirrors selections but enters on a fixed schedule to sanity-check edge vs luck, and A/B conservative vs aggressive entries to see which survives slippage. Add a daily loss cap, a stop-out cooldown (e.g., pause after 3 losers), and exposure limits per token and narrative; auto-cut if invalidation isn’t met within a time budget. Track quoted vs filled price to catch latency and liquidity holes; prefer post-only in thin books and switch to market only when RVOL is high. Polygon and Bybit handle data and execution for me, while DreamFactory sits over Postgres so the bot can audit theses vs outcomes without custom APIs. Prove the thesis tags actually add edge, and keep firm risk rails.
